lines.english: I am just a fool, Lord; I place my hopes in You! ||2||
renouncing other men's women, he practices celibacy.
The Sannyaasee smears his body with ashes;
I am just a fool - I am saved by the Lord's Name. ||3||
The Kh'shaatriya acts bravely, and is recognized as a warrior.
The Shoodra and the Vaisha work and slave for others;
I am blind - I have taken the Lord as my Support. ||4||1||39||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਭ ਤੇਰੀ ਸ੍ਰਿਸਟਿ ਤੂੰ ਆਪਿ ਰਹਿਆ ਸਮਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: sabh teree srisatt toon aap rahiaa samaaee |
lines.english: The entire Universe is Yours; You Yourself permeate and pervade it.
O Nanak, the Gurmukhs are blessed with glorious greatness.
Gauree Gwaarayree, Fourth Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਿਰਗੁਣ ਕਥਾ ਕਥਾ ਹੈ ਹਰਿ ਕੀ ॥
lines.translit: niragun kathaa kathaa hai har kee |
lines.english: The Speech of the Lord is the most sublime speech, free of any attributes.

lines.gurmukhi: ਭਜੁ ਮਿਲਿ ਸਾਧੂ ਸੰਗਤਿ ਜਨ ਕੀ ॥
lines.translit: bhaj mil saadhoo sangat jan kee |
lines.english: Vibrate on it, meditate on it, and join the Saadh Sangat, the Company of the Holy.
O Lord of the Universe, unite me with the Sat Sangat, the True Congregation.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਰੁ ਭਉਜਲੁ ਅਕਥ ਕਥਾ ਸੁਨਿ ਹਰਿ ਕੀ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: tar bhaujal akath kathaa sun har kee |1|
lines.english: Cross over the terrifying world-ocean, listening to the Unspoken Speech of the Lord. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਰਸੁ ਰਸਨਾ ਰਾਮ ਗੁਨ ਗਾਇ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: har ras rasanaa raam gun gaae |1| rahaau |
lines.english: My tongue savors the sublime essence of the Lord, singing the Lord's Glorious Praises. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਿਨ ਦਾਸਨਿ ਦਾਸ ਕਰਹੁ ਹਮ ਰਾਮਾ ॥
lines.translit: tin daasan daas karahu ham raamaa |
lines.english: please make me the slave of their slaves, Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੋ ਜਨ ਧਿਆਵਹਿ ਹਰਿ ਹਰਿ ਨਾਮਾ ॥
lines.translit: jo jan dhiaaveh har har naamaa |
lines.english: Those humble beings who meditate on the Name of the Lord, Har, Har

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੋ ਜਨੁ ਹਮਰੈ ਮਨਿ ਚਿਤਿ ਭਾਵੈ ॥
lines.translit: so jan hamarai man chit bhaavai |
lines.english: that humble servant is pleasing to my conscious mind.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੋ ਹਰਿ ਕੀ ਹਰਿ ਕਥਾ ਸੁਣਾਵੈ ॥
lines.translit: jo har kee har kathaa sunaavai |
lines.english: One who chants the Speech of the Lord
Serving Your slaves is the ultimate good deed. ||2||
Those who are blessed with great good fortune obtain the dust of the feet of the humble. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੰਤ ਜਨਾ ਸਿਉ ਪ੍ਰੀਤਿ ਬਨਿ ਆਈ ॥
lines.translit: sant janaa siau preet ban aaee |
lines.english: Those who are blessed with such pre-ordained destiny

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਨ ਕਉ ਲਿਖਤੁ ਲਿਖਿਆ ਧੁਰਿ ਪਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: jin kau likhat likhiaa dhur paaee |
lines.english: Are in love with the humble Saints.
Those humble beings, O Nanak, are absorbed in the Naam, the Name of the Lord. ||4||2||40||
The mother loves to see her son eat.
Gauree Gwaarayree, Fourth Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਤੇ ਹਰਿ ਜਨ ਹਰਿ ਮੇਲਹੁ ਹਮ ਪਿਆਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: te har jan har melahu ham piaare |
lines.english: If only I could meet those humble servants of the Lord, O my Beloved.
The fish loves to bathe in the water.
The True Guru loves to place food in the mouth of His GurSikh. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਉ ਮਿਲਿ ਬਛਰੇ ਗਊ ਪ੍ਰੀਤਿ ਲਗਾਵੈ ॥
lines.translit: jiau mil bachhare gaoo preet lagaavai |
lines.english: As the cow shows her love to her strayed calf when she finds it,

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਨ ਮਿਲਿਆ ਦੁਖ ਜਾਹਿ ਹਮਾਰੇ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: jin miliaa dukh jaeh hamaare |1| rahaau |
lines.english: Meeting with them, my sorrows depart. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਾਮਨਿ ਪ੍ਰੀਤਿ ਜਾ ਪਿਰੁ ਘਰਿ ਆਵੈ ॥
lines.translit: kaaman preet jaa pir ghar aavai |
lines.english: and as the bride shows her love for her husband when he returns home,
The rainbird loves the rainwater, falling in torrents;

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਜਨ ਪ੍ਰੀਤਿ ਜਾ ਹਰਿ ਜਸੁ ਗਾਵੈ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: har jan preet jaa har jas gaavai |2|
lines.english: so does the Lord's humble servant love to sing the Praises of the Lord. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਰ ਪ੍ਰਾਣੀ ਪ੍ਰੀਤਿ ਮਾਇਆ ਧਨੁ ਖਾਟੇ ॥
lines.translit: nar praanee preet maaeaa dhan khaatte |
lines.english: The mortal man loves to accumulate wealth and property.
The humble servant of the Lord loves to meditate on the Formless Lord. ||3||
the king loves to see his wealth on display.
The GurSikh loves to meet and embrace the Guru.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਨ ਨਾਨਕ ਪ੍ਰੀਤਿ ਸਾਧ ਪਗ ਚਾਟੇ ॥੪॥੩॥੪੧॥
lines.translit: jan naanak preet saadh pag chaatte |4|3|41|
lines.english: Servant Nanak loves to kiss the feet of the Holy. ||4||3||41||
The GurSikh loves to find satisfaction by meeting the Guru. ||1||
The hungry person loves to eat food.
Gauree Gwaarayree, Fourth Mehl:
The beggar loves to receive charity from the wealthy landlord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਦਰਸਨੁ ਦੇਹੁ ਹਰਿ ਆਸ ਤੁਮਾਰੀ ॥
lines.translit: har darasan dehu har aas tumaaree |
lines.english: O Lord, grant me the Blessed Vision of Your Darshan; I place my hopes in You, Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਰਿ ਕਿਰਪਾ ਲੋਚ ਪੂਰਿ ਹਮਾਰੀ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: kar kirapaa loch poor hamaaree |1| rahaau |
lines.english: Shower me with Your Mercy, and fulfill my longing. ||1||Pause||
The song-bird loves the sun shining in her face.
Meeting her Beloved, all her pains are left behind.
The GurSikh loves to gaze upon the Face of the Guru. ||2||
its heart blossoms forth upon seeing its mother.
The calf loves to suck its mother's milk;
The GurSikh loves to gaze upon the Face of the Guru. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਨ ਨਾਨਕ ਪ੍ਰੀਤਿ ਤ੍ਰਿਪਤਿ ਗੁਰੁ ਸਾਚਾ ॥੪॥੪॥੪੨॥
lines.translit: jan naanak preet tripat gur saachaa |4|4|42|
lines.english: Servant Nanak is fulfilled, through the Love of the True Guru. ||4||4||42||
They shall pass away, like false and transitory decorations.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹੋਰੁ ਸਭ ਪ੍ਰੀਤਿ ਮਾਇਆ ਮੋਹੁ ਕਾਚਾ ॥
lines.translit: hor sabh preet maaeaa mohu kaachaa |
lines.english: All other loves and emotional attachment to Maya are false.
